ipc_title: Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention
bns_title: Common intention
description: When a criminal act is done by several persons in furtherance of the common intention of all, each of such persons is liable for that act in the same manner as if it were done by him alone.
punishment: Same as the principal offence
key_changes: Concept retained. Moved from Section 34 to clause 3(5) as a general definition.

ipc_title: Abetment of a thing
bns_title: Abetment of a thing
description: A person abets the doing of a thing when they instigate, conspire, or intentionally aid another person to do that thing.
punishment: Same as the principal offence if committed
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment of criminal conspiracy
bns_title: Criminal conspiracy
description: When two or more persons agree to do, or cause to be done, an illegal act, or an act which is not illegal by illegal means, such an agreement is designated a criminal conspiracy.
punishment: Death/Life imprisonment if conspiracy is to commit serious offence; otherwise up to 6 months or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 120B to 61.

ipc_title: Sedition
bns_title: Acts endangering sovereignty, unity and integrity of India
description: Whoever by words, signs, visible representation, electronic communication, or otherwise, excites or attempts to excite secession or armed rebellion or subversive activities, or encourages feelings of separatist activities or endangers sovereignty or unity and integrity of India.
punishment: Life imprisonment or up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: MAJOR CHANGE: The old 'sedition' concept has been replaced with a broader 'endangering sovereignty' provision. The term 'sedition' is no longer used. The new section is more specific about what constitutes the offence.

ipc_title: Unlawful assembly
bns_title: Unlawful assembly
description: An assembly of five or more persons is designated an unlawful assembly if the common object is to commit an offence or resist the execution of law.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Joining Unlawful Assembly Armed with Deadly Weapon
ipc_title: Joining unlawful assembly armed with deadly weapon
bns_title: Joining unlawful assembly armed with deadly weapon
description: Whoever, being armed with any deadly weapon, joins an unlawful assembly.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Note: Section 144 CrPC (prohibitory orders) is now under BNSS.

ipc_title: Promoting enmity between different groups on grounds of religion, race, etc.
bns_title: Promoting enmity between different groups
description: Whoever promotes or attempts to promote feelings of enmity, hatred or ill-will between different religious, racial, language or regional groups or castes.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both; up to 5 years if in place of worship
key_changes: Now includes electronic and social media communications explicitly.

ipc_title: Giving false evidence
bns_title: Giving false evidence
description: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath or by an express provision of law to state the truth, makes any statement which is false and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, is said to give false evidence.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: False charge of offence made with intent to injure
bns_title: False charge of offence made with intent to injure
description: Whoever, with intent to cause injury to any person, institutes or causes to be instituted any criminal proceeding against that person knowing there is no just or lawful ground for such proceeding.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Intentional insult or interruption to public servant sitting in judicial proceeding
bns_title: Intentional insult or interruption to public servant sitting in judicial proceeding
description: Whoever intentionally offers any insult or causes any interruption to any public servant while such public servant is sitting in any stage of a judicial proceeding.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.1000 or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Public nuisance
bns_title: Public nuisance
description: A person is guilty of a public nuisance who does any act or is guilty of an illegal omission which causes any common injury, danger or annoyance to the public.
punishment: Fine up to Rs.1000
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Rash driving or riding on a public way
bns_title: Rash driving or riding on a public way
description: Whoever drives any vehicle, or rides, on any public way in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.1000 or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Deliberate Acts to Outrage Religious Feelings
ipc_title: Deliberate and malicious acts, intended to outrage religious feelings of any class by insulting its religion or religious beliefs
bns_title: Deliberate and malicious acts intended to outrage religious feelings
description: Whoever, with deliberate and malicious intention of outraging the religious feelings of any class of citizens, by words, either spoken or written, or by signs or by visible representations or otherwise, insults or attempts to insult the religion or the religious beliefs of that class.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Culpable homicide
bns_title: Culpable homicide
description: Whoever causes death by doing an act with the intention of causing death, or with the intention of causing such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, or with the knowledge that he is likely by such act to cause death, commits the offence of culpable homicide.
punishment: Life imprisonment or up to 10 years + fine (if not amounting to murder)
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 299 to 100.

ipc_title: Murder
bns_title: Murder
description: Culpable homicide is murder if the act is done with the intention of causing death, or if it is done with the intention of causing such bodily injury as the offender knows to be likely to cause the death of the person, or if it is done with the intention of causing bodily injury sufficient in the ordinary course of nature to cause death.
punishment: Death or Life Imprisonment + Fine
key_changes: No substantive change in definition. Section renumbered from 300 to 101.

ipc_title: Punishment for murder
bns_title: Punishment for murder
description: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Death or Life Imprisonment + Fine
key_changes: CAUTION: IPC 302 was 'Punishment for murder' but BNS 302 is now about 'Snatching'. Always verify section numbers carefully.

offence: Culpable Homicide Not Amounting to Murder
ipc_title: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder
bns_title: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder
description: If the act is done with intention - imprisonment for life or up to 10 years + fine. If done with knowledge - up to 10 years or fine or both.
punishment: Life imprisonment or up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Causing death by negligence
bns_title: Causing death by negligence
description: Whoever causes the death of any person by doing any rash or negligent act not amounting to culpable homicide.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both; up to 5 years if by registered medical practitioner
key_changes: NEW: Added specific higher punishment for registered medical practitioners causing death by negligence (up to 5 years).

ipc_title: Dowry death
bns_title: Dowry death
description: Where the death of a woman is caused by burns or bodily injury or occurs under abnormal circumstances within seven years of her marriage, and it is shown that before her death she was subjected to cruelty or harassment by her husband or any relative of her husband for, or in connection with, any demand for dowry.
punishment: Minimum 7 years up to Life Imprisonment
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 304B to 80.

ipc_title: Abetment of suicide
bns_title: Abetment of suicide
description: If any person commits suicide, whoever abets the commission of such suicide, shall be punished.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 306 to 108.

ipc_title: Attempt to murder
bns_title: Attempt to murder
description: Whoever does any act with the intention or knowledge to cause death, and the act causes hurt to any person, or if hurt is caused to any person by such act.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine; Life imprisonment if hurt is caused; Death if person under sentence of life imprisonment
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 307 to 109.

ipc_title: Hurt
bns_title: Hurt
description: Whoever causes bodily pain, disease or infirmity to any person is said to cause hurt.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine up to Rs.10000 or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Grievous hurt
bns_title: Grievous hurt
description: Emasculation, permanent privation of sight/hearing, privation of any member or joint, destruction or permanent impairing of any member or joint, permanent disfiguration of head or face, fracture or dislocation of a bone or tooth, any hurt which endangers life or which causes the sufferer to be in severe bodily pain or unable to follow ordinary pursuits for 20 days.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt
bns_title: Voluntarily causing hurt
description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt shall be punished.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine up to Rs.10000 or both
key_changes: Community service added as an alternative punishment option.

ipc_title: Voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons or means
bns_title: Voluntarily causing hurt or grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means
description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or by fire, heated substance, poison, corrosive substance, explosive, or any substance deleterious to the human body.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt
bns_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt
description: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt shall be punished.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Assault on Woman to Outrage Modesty
ipc_title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
bns_title: Assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage her modesty.
punishment: Minimum 1 year up to 5 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 354 to 74.

ipc_title: Sexual harassment and punishment for sexual harassment
bns_title: Sexual harassment
description: Physical contact and advances involving unwelcome and explicit sexual overtures, demand or request for sexual favours, showing pornography against the will, making sexually coloured remarks.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both (for physical contact); up to 1 year or fine (for remarks)
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 354A to 75.

ipc_title: Stalking
bns_title: Stalking
description: Any man who follows a woman and contacts, or attempts to contact such woman to foster personal interaction repeatedly despite a clear indication of disinterest by such woman, or monitors the use by a woman of the internet, email or any other form of electronic communication.
punishment: Up to 3 years + fine (first offence); up to 5 years + fine (subsequent)
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 354D to 78.

ipc_title: Punishment for kidnapping
bns_title: Kidnapping
description: Whoever kidnaps any person from India or from lawful guardianship.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Trafficking of person
bns_title: Trafficking of person
description: Whoever recruits, transports, harbours, transfers, or receives a person by using threats, force, coercion, abduction, fraud, deception, abuse of power, for the purpose of exploitation.
punishment: Minimum 7 years up to 10 years + fine; Life imprisonment for trafficking of minor
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 370 to 143.

ipc_title: Rape
bns_title: Rape
description: Sexual intercourse with a woman against her will, without her consent, with consent obtained under fear/misconception, when she is unable to communicate consent, or when she is under 18 years of age.
punishment: Minimum 10 years up to Life Imprisonment + fine
key_changes: No substantive change in definition. Section renumbered from 375 to 63.

ipc_title: Punishment for rape
bns_title: Punishment for rape
description: Whoever commits rape shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term not less than ten years which may extend to imprisonment for life and fine.
punishment: Minimum 10 years up to Life Imprisonment + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 376 to 64.

ipc_title: Gang rape
bns_title: Gang rape
description: Where a woman is raped by one or more persons constituting a group or acting in furtherance of a common intention, each of those persons shall be deemed to have committed the offence of rape.
punishment: Minimum 20 years up to Life Imprisonment + fine (fine to be paid to victim)
key_changes: NEW: Added provision that fine shall be paid to the victim, and shall be just and reasonable to meet medical expenses and rehabilitation.

ipc_title: Unnatural offences
bns_title: Omitted
description: This section criminalized consensual same-sex relations. It was read down by the Supreme Court in Navtej Singh Johar v. Union of India (2018) and has been omitted in BNS.
punishment: N/A - Omitted
key_changes: OMITTED: Section 377 IPC has been removed entirely from BNS, following the Supreme Court's 2018 decision decriminalizing consensual same-sex relations.

ipc_title: Theft
bns_title: Theft
description: Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any moveable property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent, moves that property.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment for theft
bns_title: Punishment for theft
description: Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment up to three years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 379 to 303(2).

ipc_title: Theft in dwelling house
bns_title: Theft in dwelling house
description: Whoever commits theft in any building, tent or vessel used as a human dwelling or for the custody of property.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Extortion
bns_title: Extortion
description: Whoever intentionally puts any person in fear of any injury to that person, or any other person, and thereby dishonestly induces the person so put in fear to deliver property.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Robbery
bns_title: Robbery
description: In all robbery there is either theft or extortion. When theft is robbery: if the offender causes or attempts to cause death, hurt, or wrongful restraint, or fear of instant death, hurt, or wrongful restraint.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine; up to 14 years if on highway between sunset and sunrise
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment for robbery
bns_title: Punishment for robbery
description: Whoever commits robbery shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term up to ten years and fine.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment for dacoity
bns_title: Dacoity
description: When five or more persons conjointly commit or attempt to commit a robbery, every person so committing, attempting or aiding is said to commit dacoity.
punishment: Life imprisonment or up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Dishonest misappropriation of property
bns_title: Dishonest misappropriation of property
description: Whoever dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use any moveable property.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Criminal breach of trust
bns_title: Criminal breach of trust
description: Whoever, being entrusted with property or with any dominion over property, dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use that property, or dishonestly uses or disposes of that property.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both; up to 7 years if by carrier, agent, banker, merchant, attorney
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 405/406 to 316.

offence: Punishment for Criminal Breach of Trust
ipc_title: Punishment for criminal breach of trust
bns_title: Criminal breach of trust
description: Whoever commits criminal breach of trust shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: Merged with definition section. Section 405 and 406 IPC now combined under BNS 316.

ipc_title: Cheating
bns_title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
description: Whoever, by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces the person so deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to consent that any person shall retain any property.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 415 to 318.

offence: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property
ipc_title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
bns_title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
description: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: The famous 'Section 420' is now BNS 318(4). No substantive change in the offence definition.

ipc_title: Mischief
bns_title: Mischief
description: Whoever with intent to cause, or knowing that he is likely to cause, wrongful loss or damage to the public or to any person, causes the destruction of any property.
punishment: Up to 3 months or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Criminal trespass
bns_title: Criminal trespass
description: Whoever enters into or upon property in the possession of another with intent to commit an offence or to intimidate, insult or annoy any person in possession of such property.
punishment: Up to 3 months or fine up to Rs.500 or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment for house-trespass
bns_title: House trespass
description: Whoever commits criminal trespass by entering into or remaining in any building, tent or vessel used as a human dwelling or any building used as a place for worship or as a place for the custody of property.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine up to Rs.1000 or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Forgery
bns_title: Forgery
description: Whoever makes any false document or false electronic record or part of a document or electronic record, with intent to cause damage or injury, or to support any claim or title.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 463 to 336.

ipc_title: Forgery of valuable security, will, etc.
bns_title: Forgery of valuable security, will, etc.
description: Whoever forges a document which purports to be a valuable security or a will, or an authority to adopt a son, or which purports to give authority to any person to make or transfer any valuable security.
punishment: Life imprisonment or up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Counterfeiting currency-notes or bank-notes
bns_title: Counterfeiting currency-notes or bank-notes
description: Whoever counterfeits, or knowingly performs any part of the process of counterfeiting, any currency-note or bank-note.
punishment: Life imprisonment or up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 489A to 344.

ipc_title: Marrying again during lifetime of husband or wife
bns_title: Marrying again during lifetime of husband or wife
description: Whoever, having a husband or wife living, marries in any case in which such marriage is void by reason of its taking place during the life of such husband or wife.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 494 to 82.

ipc_title: Adultery
bns_title: Omitted
description: This section was struck down as unconstitutional by the Supreme Court in Joseph Shine v. Union of India (2018). It has been omitted from BNS.
punishment: N/A - Omitted
key_changes: OMITTED: Section 497 IPC (Adultery) was declared unconstitutional by Supreme Court in 2018 and has not been included in BNS.

ipc_title: Husband or relative of husband of a woman subjecting her to cruelty
bns_title: Cruelty by husband or his relatives
description: Whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty shall be punished. Cruelty includes conduct which drives the woman to suicide or causes grave injury, or harassment with a view to coerce her or her relatives to meet any unlawful demand for property/dowry.
punishment: Up to 3 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 498A to 85. This remains one of the most frequently used sections in domestic violence cases.

ipc_title: Defamation
bns_title: Defamation
description: Whoever, by words either spoken or intended to be read, or by signs or by visible representations, makes or publishes any imputation concerning any person intending to harm the reputation of such person.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 499/500 to 356.

ipc_title: Criminal intimidation
bns_title: Criminal intimidation
description: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation, or property, or threatens to impute unchastity to a woman, with intent to cause alarm, or to cause that person to do or omit any act.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both; up to 7 years if threat is of death/grievous hurt/fire/offence punishable with death/life imprisonment
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 503/506 to 351.

offence: Insult to Provoke Breach of Peace
ipc_title: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of the peace
bns_title: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of the peace
description: Whoever intentionally insults, and thereby gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause him to break the public peace.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 504 to 352.

ipc_title: Punishment for criminal intimidation
bns_title: Criminal intimidation
description: Whoever commits criminal intimidation shall be punished. If the threat is of death or grievous hurt, or of fire, or of offence punishable with death or life imprisonment, the punishment is higher.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both; up to 7 years for serious threats
key_changes: Merged with definition. Section 503 and 506 IPC now combined under BNS 351.

offence: Word, Gesture or Act Intended to Insult Modesty of a Woman
ipc_title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman
bns_title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman
description: Whoever, intending to insult the modesty of any woman, utters any word, makes any sound or gesture, or exhibits any object, intending that such word, sound, gesture, or object shall be heard or seen by such woman.
punishment: Up to 3 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 509 to 79.

ipc_title: Punishment for attempting to commit offences punishable with imprisonment for life or other imprisonment
bns_title: Attempt to commit offences
description: Whoever attempts to commit an offence punishable by this Code with imprisonment for life or imprisonment, and does any act towards the commission of the offence.
punishment: Up to half the longest term of imprisonment provided for the offence, or fine, or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 511 to 62.

ipc_title: Not in IPC
bns_title: Sexual intercourse by employing deceitful means etc.
description: NEW PROVISION: Whoever, by deceitful means or by making promise to marry which he does not intend to fulfil, has sexual intercourse with a woman which is not rape.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: ENTIRELY NEW: This section has no IPC equivalent. It criminalizes sexual intercourse obtained through false promise of marriage or other deceitful means.

ipc_title: Not in IPC
bns_title: Organised crime
description: NEW PROVISION: Any continuing unlawful activity including kidnapping, extortion, contract killing, land grabbing, financial scams, cybercrime by a member of organised crime syndicate acting singly or jointly.
punishment: Death or Life imprisonment + fine of minimum Rs.10 lakh; if causing death - Death or Life imprisonment
key_changes: ENTIRELY NEW: This section has no IPC equivalent. It introduces 'organised crime' as a specific offence in central criminal law.

ipc_title: Not in IPC
bns_title: Petty organised crime
description: NEW PROVISION: Any group or gang of three or more persons committing theft, snatching, cheating, sale of stolen property, pick-pocketing, etc.
punishment: Minimum 1 year up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: ENTIRELY NEW: Targets organized petty crimes by gangs. No equivalent in IPC.

ipc_title: Not in IPC
bns_title: Terrorist act
description: NEW PROVISION: Act with intent to threaten the unity, integrity, security, sovereignty of India, to strike terror in people, using bombs, dynamite, explosive substances, firearms, biological/chemical/nuclear agents, causing death or injury.
punishment: Death or Life imprisonment; minimum 5 years for conspiracy/preparation
key_changes: ENTIRELY NEW: Previously terrorism was dealt with only under special laws (UAPA, NIA Act). BNS brings it into general criminal law for the first time.

ipc_title: Not in IPC
bns_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by mob resulting in death
description: NEW PROVISION: When a group of five or more persons acting in concert commits an offence of voluntarily causing grievous hurt on the ground of race, caste, sex, place of birth, language, personal belief or any other similar ground and such act results in death.
punishment: Death or Life imprisonment with minimum 7 years
key_changes: ENTIRELY NEW: India's first specific anti-mob-lynching provision in criminal law. No IPC equivalent existed.

ipc_title: Not in IPC
bns_title: Snatching
description: NEW PROVISION: Whoever commits theft, with the intention to steal any moveable property, by suddenly or quickly or forcibly seizing or grabbing the property from any person. CAUTION: IPC 302 was 'murder' but BNS 302 is 'snatching'.
punishment: Up to 3 years + fine
key_changes: ENTIRELY NEW: Snatching is now a separate offence. IMPORTANT: BNS 302 is NOT the same as IPC 302 (which was murder, now BNS 103).

ipc_title: Not in IPC
bns_title: Snatching with hurt
description: NEW PROVISION: Whoever, while committing snatching, causes hurt or grievous hurt or danger to life or limb of any person.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: ENTIRELY NEW: Higher punishment for snatching that causes injury.

ipc_title: Public servant disobeying direction under law
bns_title: Public servant disobeying law with intent to cause injury
description: If a public servant knowingly disobeys any direction of the law which prohibits him from requiring the attendance at any place of any person for the purpose of investigation into an offence or any other matter, or knowingly disobeys any other direction of the law regulating the manner in which he shall conduct such investigation.
punishment: Minimum 6 months up to 2 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. This section is important because it allows action against police officers who refuse to register an FIR.

ipc_title: Punishment for bribery
bns_title: Bribery
description: Whoever commits the offence of bribery shall be punished with imprisonment or fine or both.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Resistance or obstruction by a person to his lawful apprehension
bns_title: Resistance or obstruction by a person to lawful apprehension
description: Whoever intentionally offers any resistance or illegal obstruction to the lawful apprehension of himself for any offence with which he is charged or of which he has been convicted.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Sale of obscene books, etc.
bns_title: Sale of obscene material
description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, distributes, publicly exhibits or circulates any obscene book, pamphlet, paper, drawing, painting, representation or figure or any other obscene object whatsoever.
punishment: Up to 2 years + fine (first offence); up to 5 years + fine (subsequent)
key_changes: Now explicitly includes electronic and digital media. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Obscene acts and songs
bns_title: Obscene acts and songs
description: Whoever, to the annoyance of others, does any obscene act in any public place, or sings, recites or utters any obscene song, ballad or words in or near any public place.
punishment: Up to 3 months or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from IPC 294 to BNS 296.

ipc_title: Causing miscarriage
bns_title: Causing miscarriage
description: Whoever voluntarily causes a woman with child to miscarry, if such miscarriage is not caused in good faith for the purpose of saving the life of the woman.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both; up to 7 years if woman is quick with child
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered. Note: MTP Act provides lawful exceptions.

ipc_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by use of acid, etc.
bns_title: Acid attack
description: Whoever causes permanent or partial damage or deformity to, or burns or maims or disfigures or disables, any part or parts of the body of a person or causes grievous hurt by throwing acid or using acid in any form.
punishment: Minimum 10 years up to Life Imprisonment + fine (reasonable to meet medical expenses)
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 326A to 124.

ipc_title: Wrongful confinement
bns_title: Wrongful confinement
description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person in such a manner as to prevent that person from proceeding beyond certain circumscribing limits.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine up to Rs.1000 or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Assault
bns_title: Assault
description: Whoever makes any gesture, or any preparation intending or knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that criminal force is about to be used to him.
punishment: Up to 3 months or fine up to Rs.500 or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment for rape on woman under twelve years of age
bns_title: Rape on woman under twelve years of age
description: Whoever commits rape on a woman under twelve years of age shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than twenty years but which may extend to imprisonment for life.
punishment: Minimum 20 years up to Life Imprisonment or Death + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 376AB to 65(1).

offence: Rape Causing Death or Persistent Vegetative State
ipc_title: Punishment for causing death or resulting in persistent vegetative state of victim
bns_title: Punishment for rape in certain cases
description: Whoever commits rape which causes the death of the woman or causes the woman to be in a persistent vegetative state.
punishment: Minimum 20 years up to Life Imprisonment or Death
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 376A to 66.

ipc_title: Assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to disrobe
bns_title: Assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to disrobe
description: Any man who assaults or uses criminal force to any woman or abets such act with the intention of disrobing or compelling her to be naked.
punishment: Minimum 3 years up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 354B to 76.

ipc_title: Voyeurism
bns_title: Voyeurism
description: Any man who watches, or captures the image of a woman engaging in a private act in circumstances where she would usually have the expectation of not being observed.
punishment: Up to 3 years + fine (first offence); up to 7 years + fine (subsequent)
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 354C to 77.

ipc_title: House-trespass after preparation for hurt, assault, etc.
bns_title: House-trespass with preparation for assault
description: Whoever commits house-trespass, having made preparation for causing hurt to any person or for assaulting any person.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Forgery for purpose of cheating
bns_title: Forgery for purpose of cheating
description: Whoever commits forgery, intending that the document or electronic record forged shall be used for the purpose of cheating.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 468 to 339.

ipc_title: Using as genuine a forged document or electronic record
bns_title: Using as genuine a forged document
description: Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly uses as genuine any document or electronic record which he knows or has reason to believe to be a forged document.
punishment: Same as if he had forged the document
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 471 to 340.

ipc_title: Kidnapping or abducting with intent secretly and wrongfully to confine person
bns_title: Kidnapping or abducting with intent to wrongfully confine
description: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person with intent to cause that person to be secretly and wrongfully confined.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Kidnapping, abducting or inducing woman to compel her marriage, etc.
bns_title: Kidnapping or abducting woman to compel marriage
description: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any woman with intent that she may be compelled, or knowing it to be likely that she will be compelled, to marry any person against her will.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Obstructing public servant in discharge of public functions
bns_title: Obstructing public servant in discharge of public functions
description: Whoever voluntarily obstructs any public servant in the discharge of his public functions.
punishment: Up to 3 months or fine up to Rs.500 or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Assault or criminal force to deter public servant from discharge of his duty
bns_title: Assault or criminal force to deter public servant from duty
description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person being a public servant in the execution of his duty as such public servant.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Voluntarily Causing Hurt to Public Servant
ipc_title: Voluntarily causing hurt to deter public servant from his duty
bns_title: Voluntarily causing hurt to deter public servant from duty
description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt to any person being a public servant in the discharge of his duty.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Attempt to commit culpable homicide
bns_title: Attempt to commit culpable homicide
description: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of culpable homicide not amounting to murder.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both; up to 7 years if hurt is caused
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Dacoity with murder
bns_title: Dacoity with murder
description: If any one of five or more persons, who are conjointly committing dacoity, commits murder in so committing dacoity, every one of those persons shall be punished.
punishment: Death or Life Imprisonment + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Bribery
bns_title: Bribery in relation to elections
description: Whoever gives a gratification to any person with the object of inducing him or any other person to exercise any electoral right or to reward any person for having exercised any such right.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Injuring or defiling place of worship with intent to insult the religion of any class
bns_title: Injuring or defiling place of worship
description: Whoever destroys, damages or defiles any place of worship, or any object held sacred by any class of persons with the intention of thereby insulting the religion of any class.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Harbouring offender
bns_title: Harbouring offender
description: Whenever an offence has been committed, whoever harbours or conceals a person whom he knows or has reason to believe to be the offender.
punishment: Proportional to the offence of the person harboured
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishments
bns_title: Punishments
description: Lists the punishments to which offenders are liable: death, imprisonment for life, imprisonment (rigorous or simple), forfeiture of property, fine and community service.
punishment: N/A - defines the kinds of punishment
key_changes: NEW: Community service is introduced for the first time as a punishment in Indian criminal law, for petty offences such as theft of property under Rs.5000, public drunkenness, defamation and attempted suicide to compel a public servant.

offence: Act Done by a Person Bound by Law
ipc_title: Act done by a person bound by law
bns_title: Act done by a person bound by law
description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a person who is, or who by reason of a mistake of fact and not by reason of a mistake of law in good faith believes himself to be, bound by law to do it.
punishment: No offence - complete defence
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Act Done by a Person Justified by Law
ipc_title: Act done by a person justified, or by mistake of fact believing himself justified, by law
bns_title: Act done by a person justified, or by mistake of fact believing himself justified, by law
description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a person who is justified by law, or who by reason of a mistake of fact in good faith believes himself to be justified by law, in doing it.
punishment: No offence - complete defence
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Accident in doing a lawful act
bns_title: Accident in doing a lawful act
description: Nothing is an offence which is done by accident or misfortune, and without any criminal intention or knowledge, in the doing of a lawful act in a lawful manner by lawful means and with proper care and caution.
punishment: No offence - complete defence
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Act of a Child Under Seven Years
ipc_title: Act of a child under seven years of age
bns_title: Act of a child under seven years of age
description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a child under seven years of age. A child below 7 is conclusively presumed incapable of committing a crime (doli incapax).
punishment: No offence - complete defence
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 82 to 20.

offence: Act of a Child Between Seven and Twelve
ipc_title: Act of a child above seven and under twelve of immature understanding
bns_title: Act of a child above seven and under twelve years of age of immature understanding
description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a child above seven and under twelve years of age who has not attained sufficient maturity of understanding to judge the nature and consequences of his conduct on that occasion.
punishment: No offence if immaturity of understanding is proved
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered. Juvenile Justice Act procedures apply separately.

offence: Act of a Person of Unsound Mind
ipc_title: Act of a person of unsound mind
bns_title: Act of a person of unsound mind
description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a person who, at the time of doing it, by reason of unsoundness of mind, is incapable of knowing the nature of the act, or that what he is doing is either wrong or contrary to law.
punishment: No offence - complete defence (insanity)
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 84 to 22.

offence: Act of a Person Intoxicated Against His Will
ipc_title: Act of a person incapable of judgment by reason of intoxication caused against his will
bns_title: Act of a person incapable of judgment by reason of intoxication caused against his will
description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a person who, at the time of doing it, is by reason of intoxication incapable of knowing the nature of the act, provided the thing which intoxicated him was administered to him without his knowledge or against his will.
punishment: No offence - defence only for involuntary intoxication
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered. Voluntary intoxication remains no defence.

offence: Act Not Intended to Cause Death, Done by Consent
ipc_title: Act not intended and not known to be likely to cause death or grievous hurt, done by consent
bns_title: Act not intended and not known to be likely to cause death or grievous hurt, done by consent
description: Nothing which is not intended to cause death or grievous hurt is an offence by reason of any harm it may cause to a person above eighteen years of age who has given consent, express or implied, to suffer that harm.
punishment: No offence where valid consent exists
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Act Done in Good Faith for Benefit of a Person, by Consent
ipc_title: Act not intended to cause death, done by consent in good faith for person's benefit
bns_title: Act not intended to cause death, done by consent in good faith for person's benefit
description: Nothing which is not intended to cause death is an offence by reason of any harm it may cause to a person for whose benefit it is done in good faith, and who has given consent to suffer that harm. This protects doctors performing risky but consented surgery.
punishment: No offence where done in good faith with consent
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Act Done in Good Faith for Benefit of a Child or Person of Unsound Mind
ipc_title: Act done in good faith for benefit of child or insane person, by or by consent of guardian
bns_title: Act done in good faith for benefit of child or person of unsound mind, by or by consent of guardian
description: Nothing done in good faith for the benefit of a person under twelve years of age or of unsound mind, by or by consent of the guardian, is an offence by reason of any harm it may cause.
punishment: No offence where done in good faith with guardian's consent
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Consent Given Under Fear or Misconception
ipc_title: Consent known to be given under fear or misconception
bns_title: Consent known to be given under fear or misconception
description: A consent is not a valid consent if it is given by a person under fear of injury, or under a misconception of fact, or by a person of unsound mind or intoxicated, or by a person under twelve years of age.
punishment: N/A - defines when consent is invalid
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 90 to 28. Crucial in rape, cheating and medical consent cases.

ipc_title: Act causing slight harm
bns_title: Act causing slight harm
description: Nothing is an offence by reason that it causes, or that it is intended to cause, or that it is known to be likely to cause, any harm, if that harm is so slight that no person of ordinary sense and temper would complain of such harm (de minimis).
punishment: No offence - trivial harm
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Things done in private defence
bns_title: Things done in private defence
description: Nothing is an offence which is done in the exercise of the right of private defence.
punishment: No offence - complete defence
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 96 to 34.

offence: Right of Private Defence of Body and Property
ipc_title: Right of private defence of the body and of property
bns_title: Right of private defence of the body and of property
description: Every person has a right to defend his own body and the body of any other person against any offence affecting the human body, and to defend his own or another's property against theft, robbery, mischief or criminal trespass.
punishment: No offence when exercised within limits
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 97 to 35.

offence: Acts Against Which There Is No Right of Private Defence
ipc_title: Acts against which there is no right of private defence
bns_title: Acts against which there is no right of private defence
description: There is no right of private defence against an act done by a public servant acting in good faith under colour of his office, nor where there is time to have recourse to the protection of public authorities. The harm inflicted must never be more than is necessary for defence.
punishment: N/A - limits the right of private defence
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: When Private Defence of Body Extends to Causing Death
ipc_title: When the right of private defence of the body extends to causing death
bns_title: When the right of private defence of the body extends to causing death
description: The right of private defence of the body extends to voluntarily causing death where the assault reasonably causes apprehension of death, grievous hurt, rape, gratification of unnatural lust, kidnapping or abduction, or wrongful confinement in circumstances where the person cannot have recourse to public authorities.
punishment: No offence - killing in lawful private defence is justified
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 100 to 38. This is the section most often relied on in self-defence pleas.

offence: When Private Defence of Property Extends to Causing Death
ipc_title: When the right of private defence of property extends to causing death
bns_title: When the right of private defence of property extends to causing death
description: The right of private defence of property extends to causing death where the offence is robbery, house-breaking after sunset and before sunrise, mischief by fire on a dwelling, or theft/mischief/house-trespass causing reasonable apprehension of death or grievous hurt.
punishment: No offence - killing in lawful defence of property is justified in the listed cases
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 103 to 41.

offence: Private Defence Against Deadly Assault Risking an Innocent Person
ipc_title: Right of private defence against deadly assault when there is risk of harm to innocent person
bns_title: Right of private defence against deadly assault when there is risk of harm to innocent person
description: If in the exercise of the right of private defence against an assault reasonably causing apprehension of death a person is so situated that he cannot effectively exercise that right without risk of harm to an innocent person, his right of private defence extends to running that risk.
punishment: No offence - defence available even at risk to bystanders
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Sexual Intercourse by Husband During Separation
ipc_title: Sexual intercourse by husband upon his wife during separation
bns_title: Sexual intercourse by husband upon his wife during separation
description: Whoever has sexual intercourse with his own wife, who is living separately under a decree of separation or otherwise, without her consent.
punishment: Minimum 2 years up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 376B to 67.

offence: Sexual Intercourse by a Person in Authority
ipc_title: Sexual intercourse by a person in authority
bns_title: Sexual intercourse by a person in authority
description: Whoever, being in a position of authority or in a fiduciary relationship, or a public servant, superintendent of jail/remand home/hospital, or a hospital manager or staff, abuses such position to induce or seduce a woman under his charge to have sexual intercourse with him not amounting to rape.
punishment: Minimum 5 years up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 376C to 68.

ipc_title: Punishment for repeat offenders
bns_title: Punishment for repeat offenders
description: Whoever has been previously convicted of rape, gang rape or related aggravated sexual offences and is subsequently convicted again of such an offence.
punishment: Imprisonment for the remainder of natural life, or death
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 376E to 71.

offence: Disclosing Identity of a Victim of a Sexual Offence
ipc_title: Disclosure of identity of the victim of certain offences
bns_title: Disclosure of identity of victim of certain offences
description: Whoever prints or publishes the name or any matter which may make known the identity of a victim of rape or other specified sexual offences. Media, social media posts and forwards revealing a survivor's identity are covered.
punishment: Up to 2 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 228A to 72 and moved into the chapter on offences against women and children.

offence: Cohabitation Caused by Deceitful Belief of Lawful Marriage
ipc_title: Cohabitation caused by a man deceitfully inducing a belief of lawful marriage
bns_title: Cohabitation caused by a man deceitfully inducing a belief of lawful marriage
description: Every man who by deceit causes any woman who is not lawfully married to him to believe that she is lawfully married to him and to cohabit or have sexual intercourse with him in that belief.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 493 to 81.

ipc_title: Same offence with concealment of former marriage from person with whom subsequent marriage is contracted
bns_title: Marriage ceremony fraudulently gone through without lawful marriage
description: Whoever commits bigamy having concealed from the person with whom the subsequent marriage is contracted the fact of the former marriage.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 495 to 83.

ipc_title: Marriage ceremony fraudulently gone through without lawful marriage
bns_title: Enticing or taking away or detaining with criminal intent a married woman
description: Whoever dishonestly or with a fraudulent intention goes through the ceremony of being married, knowing that he is not thereby lawfully married.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: Section renumbered. Verify the exact clause before filing, as the marriage-related offences were regrouped in BNS Chapter V.

ipc_title: Explanation to Section 498A defining cruelty
bns_title: Cruelty defined
description: Cruelty means any wilful conduct of such a nature as is likely to drive the woman to commit suicide or to cause grave injury or danger to her life, limb or health (mental or physical); or harassment of the woman with a view to coercing her or any person related to her to meet any unlawful demand for property or valuable security.
punishment: Punishment is under BNS 85 (up to 3 years + fine)
key_changes: The Explanation defining cruelty is now a standalone section, BNS 86. Punishment remains under BNS 85 (old IPC 498A).

ipc_title: Causing miscarriage without woman's consent
bns_title: Causing miscarriage without woman's consent
description: Whoever causes a miscarriage without the consent of the woman, whether or not she is quick with child.
punishment: Life imprisonment or up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 313 to 89.

offence: Death Caused by an Act Done With Intent to Cause Miscarriage
ipc_title: Death caused by act done with intent to cause miscarriage
bns_title: Death caused by act done with intent to cause miscarriage
description: Whoever, with intent to cause the miscarriage of a woman with child, does any act which causes the death of such woman.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine; up to life imprisonment if done without her consent
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Act to Prevent a Child Being Born Alive
ipc_title: Act done with intent to prevent child being born alive or to cause it to die after birth
bns_title: Act done with intent to prevent child being born alive or to cause it to die after birth
description: Whoever before the birth of any child does any act with the intention of thereby preventing that child from being born alive or causing it to die after its birth, and does by such act prevent that child from being born alive or cause it to die after its birth.
punishment: Up to 10 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Causing Death of a Quick Unborn Child
ipc_title: Causing death of quick unborn child by act amounting to culpable homicide
bns_title: Causing death of quick unborn child by act amounting to culpable homicide
description: Whoever does any act under such circumstances that if he thereby caused death he would be guilty of culpable homicide, and by such act causes the death of a quick unborn child.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Exposure and Abandonment of a Child Under Twelve
ipc_title: Exposure and abandonment of child under twelve years, by parent or person having care of it
bns_title: Exposure and abandonment of child under twelve years of age by parent or person having care of it
description: Whoever, being the father or mother of a child under twelve years of age, or having the care of such child, exposes or leaves the child in any place with the intention of wholly abandoning it.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 317 to 93.

offence: Concealment of Birth by Secret Disposal of Body
ipc_title: Concealment of birth by secret disposal of dead body
bns_title: Concealment of birth by secret disposal of dead body
description: Whoever, by secretly burying or otherwise disposing of the dead body of a child, whether the child died before, during or after birth, intentionally conceals or endeavours to conceal the birth of such child.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Procuration of a Minor Girl / Child
ipc_title: Procuration of minor girl
bns_title: Procuration of child
description: Whoever, by any means, induces a child to go from any place or to do any act with intent that the child may be, or knowing that it is likely that the child will be, forced or seduced to illicit intercourse with another person.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: Widened: the offence is now gender-neutral in respect of the victim and applies to any 'child', not only a minor girl.

ipc_title: Selling minor for purposes of prostitution, etc.
bns_title: Selling child for purpose of prostitution, etc.
description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, or otherwise disposes of any child with intent that such child shall at any age be employed or used for the purpose of prostitution or illicit intercourse or for any unlawful and immoral purpose.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: Made gender-neutral in respect of the victim - covers any child, not only a minor girl. Section renumbered from 372 to 98.

ipc_title: Buying minor for purposes of prostitution, etc.
bns_title: Buying child for purpose of prostitution, etc.
description: Whoever buys, hires or otherwise obtains possession of any child with the intent that such child shall be employed or used for the purpose of prostitution or illicit intercourse or for any unlawful and immoral purpose.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: Made gender-neutral in respect of the victim. Section renumbered from 373 to 99.

offence: Culpable Homicide by Causing Death of a Person Other Than Intended
ipc_title: Culpable homicide by causing death of person other than person whose death was intended
bns_title: Culpable homicide by causing death of person other than person whose death was intended
description: If a person, by doing anything which he intends or knows to be likely to cause death, causes the death of any person whose death he neither intends nor knows himself to be likely to cause, the culpable homicide committed is of the description of which it would have been if he had caused the death of the person whose death he intended (doctrine of transferred malice).
punishment: Same as for the intended offence
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment for murder by life-convict
bns_title: Punishment for murder by life-convict
description: Whoever, being under sentence of imprisonment for life, commits murder.
punishment: Death or imprisonment for the remainder of that person's natural life
key_changes: IMPORTANT: The mandatory death sentence in old IPC 303 was struck down (Mithu v. State of Punjab, 1983). BNS 104 gives the court a choice between death and imprisonment for the remainder of natural life.

offence: Abetment of Suicide of a Child or Person of Unsound Mind
ipc_title: Abetment of suicide of child or insane person
bns_title: Abetment of suicide of child or person of unsound mind
description: If any person under eighteen years of age, any person of unsound mind, any delirious person or any person in a state of intoxication commits suicide, whoever abets the commission of such suicide.
punishment: Death, or life imprisonment, or up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 305 to 107.

ipc_title: Attempt to commit suicide
bns_title: Omitted
description: Attempt to suicide is no longer a general offence. Section 115 of the Mental Healthcare Act, 2017 already presumes severe stress and bars prosecution, and BNS does not re-enact IPC 309. A person attempting suicide must be given care, not charged. (BNS 226 separately covers attempting suicide to compel or restrain a public servant from doing his duty.)
punishment: N/A - Omitted as a general offence
key_changes: OMITTED: The general offence of attempt to suicide is gone. If you or someone you know needs help, call Tele-MANAS 14416 or KIRAN 1800-599-0019.

ipc_title: Voluntarily causing hurt
bns_title: Voluntarily causing hurt
description: Whoever does any act with the intention of thereby causing hurt to any person, or with the knowledge that he is likely thereby to cause hurt to any person, and does thereby cause hurt to any person, is said voluntarily to cause hurt.
punishment: Punishment under BNS 115(2): up to 1 year or fine up to Rs.10000 or both, or community service
key_changes: Definition and punishment merged into a single section, BNS 115.

ipc_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt
bns_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt
description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt, if the hurt which he intends to cause or knows himself to be likely to cause is grievous hurt, and if the hurt which he causes is grievous hurt, is said voluntarily to cause grievous hurt.
punishment: Punishment under BNS 117(2): up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: Definition and punishment merged into a single section, BNS 117.

ipc_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means
bns_title: Voluntarily causing hurt or grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means
description: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or by fire, heated substance, poison, corrosive substance, explosive, or any substance deleterious to the human body.
punishment: Life imprisonment, or minimum 1 year up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: Merged with old IPC 324 into a single section, BNS 118: sub-section (1) covers hurt, sub-section (2) covers grievous hurt.

ipc_title: Voluntarily throwing or attempting to throw acid
bns_title: Attempt to throw or administer acid
description: Whoever throws or attempts to throw acid on any person, or attempts to administer acid to any person, with the intention or knowledge that permanent or partial damage or deformity or burns or maiming or disfigurement or disability or grievous hurt is likely to be caused.
punishment: Minimum 5 years up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 326B to 124(2). The acid attack offence itself is BNS 124(1).

ipc_title: Voluntarily causing hurt to extort property, or to constrain to an illegal act
bns_title: Voluntarily causing hurt to extort property, or to constrain to an illegal act
description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt for the purpose of extorting from the sufferer, or from any person interested in the sufferer, any property or valuable security, or of constraining the sufferer or any person interested to do anything illegal or which may facilitate the commission of an offence.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Causing hurt by means of poison, etc., with intent to commit an offence
bns_title: Hurt caused by means of poison, etc., with intent to commit an offence
description: Whoever administers to or causes any person to take any poison, stupefying, intoxicating or unwholesome drug or other thing with intent to cause hurt, or to commit or facilitate an offence. Commonly used in drink-spiking and drug-facilitated robbery cases.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 328 to 123.

offence: Causing Hurt to Extort a Confession
ipc_title: Voluntarily causing hurt to extort confession, or to compel restoration of property
bns_title: Voluntarily causing hurt to extort confession, or to compel restoration of property
description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt for the purpose of extorting from the sufferer, or from any person interested in the sufferer, any confession or information which may lead to the detection of an offence, or to compel the restoration of property. This is the main provision used against custodial torture by police.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 330 to 120(1).

offence: Causing Grievous Hurt to Extort a Confession
ipc_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort confession, or to compel restoration of property
bns_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort confession, or to compel restoration of property
description: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt for the purpose of extorting a confession or information which may lead to the detection of an offence, or to compel restoration of property. Applies to serious custodial torture.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 331 to 120(2).

offence: Act Endangering Life or Personal Safety
ipc_title: Act endangering life or personal safety of others
bns_title: Act endangering life or personal safety of others
description: Whoever does any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or the personal safety of others. Frequently applied to dangerous driving, unsafe construction sites and reckless handling of machinery.
punishment: Up to 3 months or fine up to Rs.2500 or both
key_changes: MERGED: Old IPC 336, 337 and 338 are consolidated into BNS 125. Sub-clause (a) covers hurt, sub-clause (b) covers grievous hurt.

offence: Causing Hurt by an Act Endangering Life
ipc_title: Causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others
bns_title: Act endangering life or personal safety of others - causing hurt
description: Whoever causes hurt to any person by doing any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or the personal safety of others.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: MERGED into BNS 125 along with old IPC 336 and 338.

offence: Causing Grievous Hurt by an Act Endangering Life
ipc_title: Causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others
bns_title: Act endangering life or personal safety of others - causing grievous hurt
description: Whoever causes grievous hurt to any person by doing any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or the personal safety of others.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine up to Rs.10000 or both
key_changes: MERGED into BNS 125 along with old IPC 336 and 337.

ipc_title: Wrongful restraint
bns_title: Wrongful restraint
description: Whoever voluntarily obstructs any person so as to prevent that person from proceeding in any direction in which that person has a right to proceed, is said wrongfully to restrain that person.
punishment: Punishment under BNS 126(2): up to 1 month or fine up to Rs.5000 or both, or community service
key_changes: Definition and punishment merged into BNS 126.

ipc_title: Punishment for wrongful restraint
bns_title: Wrongful restraint - punishment
description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished. Commonly used where a landlord, employer or neighbour physically blocks a person's free movement.
punishment: Up to 1 month or fine up to Rs.5000 or both, or community service
key_changes: Community service added as an alternative punishment. Merged into BNS 126(2).

ipc_title: Punishment for wrongful confinement
bns_title: Wrongful confinement - punishment
description: Whoever wrongfully confines any person shall be punished. Applies for example where an employer locks in workers or a hostel/employer confines a person against their will.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine up to Rs.5000 or both, or community service
key_changes: Community service added as an alternative punishment. Merged into BNS 127.

ipc_title: Criminal force
bns_title: Criminal force
description: Whoever intentionally uses force to any person, without that person's consent, in order to the committing of any offence, or intending or knowing it to be likely that by the use of such force he will cause injury, fear or annoyance to the person to whom the force is used.
punishment: Punishment under BNS 131
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Punishment for Assault or Criminal Force
ipc_title: Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation
bns_title: Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation
description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person.
punishment: Up to 3 months or fine up to Rs.1000 or both, or community service
key_changes: Community service added as an alternative punishment. Section renumbered from 352 to 131.

ipc_title: Assault or criminal force with intent to dishonour a person, otherwise than on grave provocation
bns_title: Assault or criminal force with intent to dishonour a person
description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, intending thereby to dishonour that person, otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Assault in Attempt to Commit Theft of Property Worn
ipc_title: Assault or criminal force in attempt to commit theft of property carried by a person
bns_title: Assault or criminal force in attempt to commit theft of property carried by a person
description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person in attempting to commit theft of any property which that person is then wearing or carrying.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered. Note that BNS also creates a separate offence of snatching.

offence: Assault in Attempt to Wrongfully Confine
ipc_title: Assault or criminal force in attempt wrongfully to confine a person
bns_title: Assault or criminal force in attempt wrongfully to confine a person
description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person in attempting wrongfully to confine that person.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Assault on Grave and Sudden Provocation
ipc_title: Assault or criminal force on grave provocation
bns_title: Assault or criminal force on grave and sudden provocation
description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person on grave and sudden provocation given by that person.
punishment: Up to 1 month or fine up to Rs.1000 or both, or community service
key_changes: Community service added as an alternative punishment. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Kidnapping (from India and from lawful guardianship)
bns_title: Kidnapping
description: Kidnapping is of two kinds: kidnapping from India (conveying a person beyond the limits of India without consent) and kidnapping from lawful guardianship (taking or enticing a minor under 18 or a person of unsound mind out of the keeping of the lawful guardian without consent).
punishment: Punishment under BNS 137(2): up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: MERGED: Old IPC 359, 360, 361 and 363 are consolidated into BNS 137. The age of a minor is now uniformly eighteen years for both boys and girls.

ipc_title: Abduction
bns_title: Abduction
description: Whoever by force compels, or by any deceitful means induces, any person to go from any place, is said to abduct that person. Abduction by itself is not punishable; it becomes an offence when done with one of the intents specified in the following sections.
punishment: N/A by itself - punishable when coupled with a specified intent
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 362 to 138.

ipc_title: Kidnapping for ransom, etc.
bns_title: Kidnapping or abducting in order to murder or for ransom, etc.
description: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person and keeps them in detention and threatens to cause death or hurt, or by their conduct gives rise to a reasonable apprehension that the person may be put to death or hurt, in order to compel the Government, a foreign State or any person to pay a ransom.
punishment: Death or Life Imprisonment + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 364A to BNS 140(2).

ipc_title: Exploitation of a trafficked person
bns_title: Exploitation of a trafficked child or person
description: Whoever, knowingly or having reason to believe that a child or person has been trafficked, engages such child or person in any manner for sexual exploitation.
punishment: Minimum 5 years up to 7 years + fine (adult victim); minimum 5 years up to 10 years + fine (child victim)
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 370A to 144. Human trafficking helpline: 1098 (Childline) / 112.

ipc_title: Unlawful compulsory labour
bns_title: Unlawful compulsory labour
description: Whoever unlawfully compels any person to labour against the will of that person. This is the criminal-law counterpart of the constitutional ban on forced labour (Article 23) and applies to bonded labour situations.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine or both, or community service
key_changes: Community service added as an alternative punishment. Section renumbered from 374 to 146. Bonded labour also attracts the Bonded Labour System (Abolition) Act, 1976.

offence: Theft by a Clerk or Servant
ipc_title: Theft by clerk or servant of property in possession of master
bns_title: Theft by clerk or servant of property in possession of master
description: Whoever, being a clerk or servant, or being employed in the capacity of a clerk or servant, commits theft in respect of any property in the possession of his master or employer.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Theft After Preparation to Cause Death or Hurt
ipc_title: Theft after preparation made for causing death, hurt or restraint in order to committing of theft
bns_title: Theft after preparation made for causing death, hurt or restraint in order to the committing of theft
description: Whoever commits theft, having made preparation for causing death, or hurt, or restraint, or fear of death, hurt or restraint, to any person, in order to the committing of such theft, or in order to effecting his escape after committing it.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment for extortion
bns_title: Extortion - punishment
description: Whoever commits extortion shall be punished. Frequently used against protection-money demands, blackmail and threats to leak private photographs or videos unless money is paid.
punishment: Up to 7 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 384 to BNS 308(2). For online sextortion, also report on cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930.

offence: Robbery or Dacoity With Attempt to Cause Death or Grievous Hurt
ipc_title: Robbery or dacoity, with attempt to cause death or grievous hurt
bns_title: Robbery or dacoity, with attempt to cause death or grievous hurt
description: If, at the time of committing robbery or dacoity, the offender uses any deadly weapon, or causes grievous hurt to any person, or attempts to cause death or grievous hurt to any person.
punishment: Minimum 7 years imprisonment
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Belonging to a Gang of Dacoits
ipc_title: Punishment for belonging to gang of dacoits
bns_title: Punishment for belonging to gang of dacoits
description: Whoever, at any time after the commencement of this Sanhita, belongs to a gang of persons associated for the purpose of habitually committing dacoity.
punishment: Life imprisonment, or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Misappropriation of Property of a Deceased Person
ipc_title: Dishonest misappropriation of property possessed by deceased person at the time of his death
bns_title: Dishonest misappropriation of property possessed by deceased person at the time of his death
description: Whoever dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use property, knowing that such property was in the possession of a deceased person at the time of that person's decease, and has not since been in the possession of any person legally entitled to such possession.
punishment: Up to 3 years + fine; up to 7 years if by a clerk or servant of the deceased
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Criminal Breach of Trust by a Public Servant, Banker or Agent
ipc_title: Criminal breach of trust by public servant, or by banker, merchant or agent
bns_title: Criminal breach of trust by public servant, or by banker, merchant or agent
description: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property in his capacity as a public servant, or in the way of his business as a banker, merchant, factor, broker, attorney or agent, commits criminal breach of trust in respect of that property.
punishment: Life imprisonment, or up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change in substance. Merged into BNS 316 as an aggravated sub-section. Verify the exact sub-clause before filing.

ipc_title: Stolen property
bns_title: Stolen property
description: Property, the possession whereof has been transferred by theft, extortion, robbery, criminal misappropriation or criminal breach of trust, is designated as stolen property, whether the transfer has been made or the misappropriation committed within or outside India.
punishment: N/A - definition
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Dishonestly receiving stolen property
bns_title: Dishonestly receiving stolen property
description: Whoever dishonestly receives or retains any stolen property, knowing or having reason to believe the same to be stolen property. Buying a suspiciously cheap phone or vehicle without papers can attract this section.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 411 to BNS 317(2).

ipc_title: Habitually dealing in stolen property
bns_title: Habitually dealing in stolen property
description: Whoever habitually receives or deals in property which he knows or has reason to believe to be stolen property.
punishment: Life imprisonment, or up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Merged into BNS 317. Verify the exact sub-clause before filing.

ipc_title: Cheating by personation
bns_title: Cheating by personation
description: A person is said to cheat by personation if he cheats by pretending to be some other person, or by knowingly substituting one person for another, or representing that he or any other person is a person other than he or such other person really is.
punishment: Punishment under BNS 319(2): up to 5 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 416 to BNS 319(1). Widely used in impersonation frauds, fake customer-care calls and fake matrimonial or job profiles.

ipc_title: Punishment for cheating
bns_title: Cheating - punishment
