description: Whoever cheats shall be punished. This is the basic punishment for cheating where no delivery of property is induced.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Merged into BNS 318.

ipc_title: Punishment for cheating by personation
bns_title: Cheating by personation - punishment
description: Whoever cheats by personation shall be punished. Applies to online impersonation frauds, fake bank/KYC calls, fake police calls and 'digital arrest' scams.
punishment: Up to 5 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 419 to BNS 319(2). Report online frauds on cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930 within the golden hour.

ipc_title: Punishment for mischief
bns_title: Mischief - punishment
description: Whoever commits mischief shall be punished. BNS grades the punishment by the value of the loss or damage caused.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine or both (loss under Rs.20000); higher for greater loss
key_changes: CHANGED: Punishment for mischief is now graded by the amount of loss/damage caused (thresholds around Rs.20000 and Rs.1 lakh). Community service is available for minor mischief.

ipc_title: Mischief causing damage to the amount of fifty rupees
bns_title: Mischief
description: Whoever commits mischief and thereby causes loss or damage to any property. The old fifty-rupee threshold has been replaced with modern value slabs.
punishment: Graded by the amount of loss/damage caused - up to 2 years or fine or both for higher-value damage
key_changes: CHANGED: The obsolete Rs.50 threshold is gone. BNS 324 grades punishment by value of damage. Verify the exact sub-clause before filing.

offence: Mischief by Fire or Explosive Substance
ipc_title: Mischief by fire or explosive substance with intent to cause damage
bns_title: Mischief by fire or explosive substance with intent to cause damage
description: Whoever commits mischief by fire or any explosive substance intending to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, damage to any property.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Consolidated in BNS 326. Verify the exact sub-clause before filing.

offence: Mischief by Fire to Destroy a House
ipc_title: Mischief by fire or explosive substance with intent to destroy house, etc.
bns_title: Mischief by fire or explosive substance with intent to destroy house
description: Whoever commits mischief by fire or any explosive substance intending to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, the destruction of any building which is ordinarily used as a place of worship, or as a human dwelling, or as a place for the custody of property.
punishment: Life imprisonment, or up to 10 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Consolidated in BNS 326. Verify the exact sub-clause before filing.

ipc_title: House-trespass
bns_title: House-trespass and house-breaking
description: Whoever commits criminal trespass by entering into, or remaining in, any building, tent or vessel used as a human dwelling or any building used as a place for worship or as a place for the custody of property, is said to commit house-trespass.
punishment: Punishment under BNS 331
key_changes: MERGED: The definitions of house-trespass, lurking house-trespass and house-breaking (old IPC 442-446) are consolidated into BNS 330.

ipc_title: Punishment for criminal trespass
bns_title: Criminal trespass - punishment
description: Whoever commits criminal trespass shall be punished. Commonly used where a person forcibly enters or refuses to leave someone else's land, shop or house.
punishment: Up to 3 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: No substantive change; fine amount modernised. Merged into BNS 329.

ipc_title: House-trespass in order to commit offence punishable with death / imprisonment
bns_title: House-trespass in order to commit an offence
description: Whoever commits house-trespass in order to the committing of any offence. The punishment is graded according to the seriousness of the offence intended.
punishment: Up to 10 years + fine; life imprisonment where the intended offence is punishable with death
key_changes: MERGED: Old IPC 449, 450, 451, 454 and 457 are consolidated into BNS 332, graded by the intended offence. Verify the exact sub-clause before filing.

ipc_title: Making a false document
bns_title: Making a false document
description: A person is said to make a false document who dishonestly or fraudulently makes, signs, seals or executes a document or electronic record, or transmits any electronic record or affixes an electronic signature, with the intention of causing it to be believed that it was made by a person by whom he knows it was not made.
punishment: N/A - definition; forgery is punishable under BNS 336
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 464 to 335. Digital signatures and electronic records are expressly covered.

ipc_title: Punishment for forgery
bns_title: Forgery - punishment
description: Whoever commits forgery shall be punished. Applies to forged rent agreements, forged sale deeds, forged salary slips, forged mark sheets and forged signatures.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Merged into BNS 336.

offence: Forgery of a Court Record or Public Register
ipc_title: Forgery of record of Court or of public register, etc.
bns_title: Forgery of record of Court or of public register, etc.
description: Whoever forges a document or an electronic record purporting to be a record or proceeding of a Court of Justice, or a register of birth, baptism, marriage or burial, or a register kept by a public servant, or a certificate or document purporting to be made by a public servant.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 466 to 337.

ipc_title: Falsification of accounts
bns_title: Falsification of accounts
description: Whoever, being a clerk, officer or servant, wilfully and with intent to defraud destroys, alters, mutilates or falsifies any book, electronic record, paper, writing, valuable security or account which belongs to his employer, or makes a false entry therein.
punishment: Up to 7 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 477A to 344. Electronic records expressly covered.

offence: Negligent Act Likely to Spread Infection
ipc_title: Negligent act likely to spread infection of disease dangerous to life
bns_title: Negligent act likely to spread infection of disease dangerous to life
description: Whoever unlawfully or negligently does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Malignant Act Likely to Spread Infection
ipc_title: Malignant act likely to spread infection of disease dangerous to life
bns_title: Malignant act likely to spread infection of disease dangerous to life
description: Whoever malignantly does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Adulteration of food or drink intended for sale
bns_title: Adulteration of food or drink intended for sale
description: Whoever adulterates any article of food or drink so as to make it noxious as food or drink, intending to sell it as food or drink, or knowing it to be likely that it will be sold as food or drink.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Fine amount modernised. Section renumbered. The Food Safety and Standards Act, 2006 also applies - complain on 1800-112-100 (FSSAI).

offence: Sale of Noxious Food or Drink
ipc_title: Sale of noxious food or drink
bns_title: Sale of noxious food or drink
description: Whoever sells, or offers or exposes for sale, as food or drink, any article which has been rendered or has become noxious, or is in a state unfit for food or drink, knowing or having reason to believe that it is noxious as food or drink.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Fine amount modernised. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Adulteration of drugs
bns_title: Adulteration of drugs
description: Whoever adulterates any drug or medical preparation in such a manner as to lessen its efficacy, or to change its operation, or to render it noxious, intending that it shall be sold or used for any medicinal purpose.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Fine amount modernised. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Sale of adulterated drugs
bns_title: Sale of adulterated drugs
description: Whoever, knowing any drug or medical preparation to have been adulterated, sells it, or offers or exposes it for sale, or issues it from any dispensary for medicinal purposes as unadulterated.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Fine amount modernised. Section renumbered.

offence: Fouling Water of a Public Spring or Reservoir
ipc_title: Fouling water of public spring or reservoir
bns_title: Fouling water of public spring or reservoir
description: Whoever voluntarily corrupts or fouls the water of any public spring or reservoir so as to render it less fit for the purpose for which it is ordinarily used.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Fine amount modernised. Section renumbered.

offence: Making the Atmosphere Noxious to Health
ipc_title: Making atmosphere noxious to health
bns_title: Making atmosphere noxious to health
description: Whoever voluntarily vitiates the atmosphere in any place so as to make it noxious to the health of persons in general dwelling or carrying on business in the neighbourhood or passing along a public way.
punishment: Fine up to Rs.1000
key_changes: Section renumbered. Air pollution is also dealt with under the Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981.

offence: Danger or Obstruction in a Public Way
ipc_title: Danger or obstruction in public way or line of navigation
bns_title: Danger or obstruction in public way or line of navigation
description: Whoever, by doing any act, or by omitting to take order with any property in his possession or under his charge, causes danger, obstruction or injury to any person in any public way or public line of navigation. Commonly used against illegal encroachments and open manholes or trenches left unguarded.
punishment: Fine up to Rs.5000
key_changes: Fine amount modernised. Section renumbered.

offence: Negligent Conduct With a Poisonous Substance
ipc_title: Negligent conduct with respect to poisonous substance
bns_title: Negligent conduct with respect to poisonous substance
description: Whoever does, with any poisonous substance, any act in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Fine amount modernised. Section renumbered.

offence: Negligent Conduct With Fire or Combustible Matter
ipc_title: Negligent conduct with respect to fire or combustible matter
bns_title: Negligent conduct with respect to fire or combustible matter
description: Whoever does, with fire or any combustible matter, any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person. Used in fire-safety violation cases.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Fine amount modernised. Section renumbered.

offence: Negligent Conduct With an Explosive Substance
ipc_title: Negligent conduct with respect to explosive substance
bns_title: Negligent conduct with respect to explosive substance
description: Whoever does, with any explosive substance, any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person. Frequently used in firecracker and illegal explosives cases.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Fine amount modernised. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Negligent conduct with respect to machinery
bns_title: Negligent conduct with respect to machinery
description: Whoever does, with any machinery, any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or knowingly or negligently omits to take such order with any machinery in his possession or under his care as is sufficient to guard against probable danger to human life. Relevant to unsafe factory and construction machinery.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Fine amount modernised. Section renumbered.

offence: Negligent Conduct in Pulling Down or Repairing Buildings
ipc_title: Negligent conduct with respect to pulling down or repairing buildings
bns_title: Negligent conduct with respect to pulling down, repairing or constructing buildings
description: Whoever, in pulling down, repairing or constructing any building, knowingly or negligently omits to take such order with that building as is sufficient to guard against any probable danger to human life from its fall. Used against unsafe demolition and construction sites.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Expanded to expressly include constructing buildings, not only pulling down or repairing them.

ipc_title: Negligent conduct with respect to animal
bns_title: Negligent conduct with respect to animal
description: Whoever knowingly or negligently omits to take such order with any animal in his possession as is sufficient to guard against any probable danger to human life, or any probable danger of grievous hurt from such animal. Used in dog-bite and stray-cattle cases against the owner.
punishment: Up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Fine amount modernised. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment for public nuisance in cases not otherwise provided for
bns_title: Punishment for public nuisance in cases not otherwise provided for
description: Whoever commits a public nuisance in any case not otherwise punishable shall be punished.
punishment: Fine up to Rs.1000
key_changes: Section renumbered. Note that BNSS 152 and 163 give a magistrate power to order removal of a nuisance.

offence: Sale of Obscene Objects to a Child
ipc_title: Sale, etc., of obscene objects to young person
bns_title: Sale, etc., of obscene objects to child
description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, distributes, exhibits or circulates to any child any obscene object, or offers or attempts to do so.
punishment: Up to 3 years + fine up to Rs.2000 (first conviction); up to 7 years + fine up to Rs.5000 (subsequent)
key_changes: Terminology updated from 'young person' to 'child'. Section renumbered. The POCSO Act, 2012 applies in addition for sexual material involving children.

ipc_title: Punishment for defamation
bns_title: Defamation - punishment
description: Whoever defames another shall be punished. Defamation is a non-cognizable, bailable offence - the police normally will not register an FIR; you file a private complaint before a magistrate.
punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both, or community service
key_changes: NEW: Community service is now available as a punishment for defamation. Merged into BNS 356.

ipc_title: Printing or engraving matter known to be defamatory
bns_title: Printing or engraving matter known to be defamatory
description: Whoever prints or engraves any matter, knowing or having good reason to believe that such matter is defamatory of any person.
punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both
key_changes: Merged into BNS 356. Verify the exact sub-clause before filing.

ipc_title: Statements conducing to public mischief
bns_title: Statements conducing to public mischief
description: Whoever makes, publishes or circulates any statement, false information, rumour or report, including through electronic means, with intent to cause fear or alarm to the public, or to induce any person to commit an offence against the State or public tranquillity, or to promote enmity or hatred between classes.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both; up to 5 years + fine if in a place of worship
key_changes: Now expressly covers false information circulated by electronic means, including social media forwards and WhatsApp rumours.

offence: Act Caused by Inducing Belief of Divine Displeasure
ipc_title: Act caused by inducing person to believe that he will be rendered an object of Divine displeasure
bns_title: Act caused by inducing person to believe that he will be rendered an object of divine displeasure
description: Whoever voluntarily causes or attempts to cause any person to do anything which that person is not legally bound to do, by inducing that person to believe that he will be rendered by some act of the offender an object of divine displeasure. Used against coercion by godmen and fake exorcists.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered.

offence: Misconduct in Public by a Drunken Person
ipc_title: Misconduct in public by a drunken person
bns_title: Misconduct in public by a drunken person
description: Whoever, in a state of intoxication, appears in any public place and there conducts himself in such a manner as to cause annoyance to any person.
punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 24 hours, or fine up to Rs.1000, or both, or community service
key_changes: NEW: Community service is now available as a punishment. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Fabricating false evidence
bns_title: Fabricating false evidence
description: Whoever causes any circumstance to exist, or makes any false entry in any book or record or electronic record, or makes any document or electronic record containing a false statement, intending that it may appear in evidence in a judicial proceeding and cause any person to form an erroneous opinion.
punishment: Punishment under BNS 229
key_changes: Expressly extended to electronic records. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Punishment for false evidence
bns_title: Punishment for false evidence
description: Whoever intentionally gives false evidence in any stage of a judicial proceeding, or fabricates false evidence for the purpose of being used in any stage of a judicial proceeding.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine (judicial proceeding); up to 3 years + fine (other cases)
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 193 to 229.

offence: Giving False Information to a Public Servant
ipc_title: False information, with intent to cause public servant to use his lawful power to the injury of another person
bns_title: False information, with intent to cause public servant to use his lawful power to the injury of another person
description: Whoever gives to any public servant any information which he knows or believes to be false, intending thereby to cause that public servant to do or omit anything which he ought not to do, or to use his lawful power to the injury or annoyance of any person. This is the section used against knowingly false police complaints.
punishment: Up to 1 year or fine up to Rs.10000 or both, or community service
key_changes: NEW: Community service is now available as a punishment. Fine modernised. Section renumbered.

offence: Disobedience to an Order of a Public Servant
ipc_title: Disobedience to order duly promulgated by public servant
bns_title: Disobedience to order duly promulgated by public servant
description: Whoever, knowing that by an order promulgated by a public servant he is directed to abstain from a certain act, disobeys such direction. This is the section used to prosecute breaches of prohibitory orders (formerly 'Section 144 CrPC', now BNSS 163).
punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs.2500 or both; up to 1 year if danger to human life is caused
key_changes: Fine modernised and community service added for the lesser offence. Section renumbered from 188 to 223.

ipc_title: Causing disappearance of evidence of offence, or giving false information to screen offender
bns_title: Causing disappearance of evidence of offence, or giving false information to screen offender
description: Whoever, knowing or having reason to believe that an offence has been committed, causes any evidence of the commission of that offence to disappear, with the intention of screening the offender from legal punishment, or gives false information respecting that offence.
punishment: Up to 7 years + fine (if the offence is capital); graded down for lesser offences
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 201 to 238.

offence: Destruction of a Document to Prevent Its Production as Evidence
ipc_title: Destruction of document or electronic record to prevent its production as evidence
bns_title: Destruction of document or electronic record to prevent its production as evidence
description: Whoever secretes or destroys any document or electronic record which he may be lawfully compelled to produce as evidence, or obliterates its contents, with the intention of preventing it from being produced or used as evidence.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: Expressly covers electronic records, including deleted chats and files. Section renumbered.

offence: Public Servant Disobeying the Law to Cause Injury
ipc_title: Public servant disobeying law, with intent to cause injury to any person
bns_title: Public servant disobeying law, with intent to cause injury to any person
description: Whoever, being a public servant, knowingly disobeys any direction of the law as to the way in which he is to conduct himself as such public servant, intending to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will by such disobedience cause, injury to any person.
punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 166 to 198. Useful against officials who wilfully ignore mandatory procedure.

offence: Public Servant Framing an Incorrect Document
ipc_title: Public servant framing an incorrect document with intent to cause injury
bns_title: Public servant framing an incorrect document with intent to cause injury
description: Whoever, being a public servant and being charged with the preparation of any document or electronic record, frames or translates it in a manner which he knows or believes to be incorrect, intending thereby to cause injury to any person.
punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both
key_changes: Expressly covers electronic records. Section renumbered.

ipc_title: Personating a public servant
bns_title: Personating a public servant
description: Whoever pretends to hold any particular office as a public servant, knowing that he does not hold such office, and in such assumed character does any act under colour of such office. Used against fake police, fake income-tax officers and 'digital arrest' scam callers.
punishment: Up to 2 years or fine or both
key_changes: No substantive change. Section renumbered from 170 to 204. If you receive such a call, hang up and report on 1930 / cybercrime.gov.in.

offence: Wearing the Garb of a Public Servant With Fraudulent Intent
ipc_title: Wearing garb or carrying token used by public servant with fraudulent intent
bns_title: Wearing garb or carrying token used by public servant with fraudulent intent
description: Whoever, not belonging to a certain class of public servants, wears any garb or carries any token resembling that used by that class of public servants, with the intention that it may be believed that he belongs to that class.
punishment: Up to 3 months or fine up to Rs.5000 or both
key_changes: Fine modernised. Section renumbered.
